overview.line: Through as group winners; first place is already secured.
A point against South Africa guarantees a top-two finish.
Needs to beat Mexico, and even then needs the other result to help.
Must beat Korea Republic and depends on the Czechia–Mexico match.

Mexico.text: Through to the round of 32 as group winners.
First place is locked regardless of the final match with Czechia. As winners, Mexico meet one of the best third-placed teams.

Korea Republic.text: A draw or win against South Africa secures qualification.
The result against South Africa settles it; the Czechia–Mexico match makes no difference.

breakdown.text: Out of the top two, left to the best-third race.

Korea Republic.text: A draw that leaves Korea Republic level with Czechia on four points is settled by the 2-1 win over Czechia, which keeps them ahead.
A defeat would leave them third on three points. That is roughly the qualifying bubble, so it would hinge on how the other groups finish.

Czechia.text: Still alive, but only by beating Mexico.
A win is not enough on its own — the South Africa–Korea Republic result must also fall Czechia's way.

breakdown.text: In the running for second, decided by the other match and goal difference.

Czechia.text: If a win leaves teams level on points for second, goal difference is the lever, and the final scorelines can still move it.

South Africa.text: Alive only with a win over Korea Republic, and even that may not be enough.
Only a win keeps South Africa in it, and it still needs the Czechia–Mexico result to help.

breakdown.text: Into the second-place picture, but it hinges on the other match and goal difference.

South Africa.text: A points tie for second would fall to goal difference, where South Africa's -2 trails its rivals.

overview.line: A draw with Switzerland is enough, most likely as winners.
A draw with Canada guarantees a top-two place.
Must beat Qatar and hope the top two slip.
A long shot: needs a big win and favours elsewhere.

Canada.text: A point against Switzerland secures qualification.
The Switzerland match is in Canada's hands; only a defeat opens any risk.

breakdown.text: Second or third, depending on Bosnia–Qatar and goal difference.

Canada.text: First place is decided directly against Switzerland. As runner-up, Canada would meet Group A's runner-up in the round of 32.
A draw leaves both on five points; Canada's +6 goal difference outranks Switzerland's +3 for first.

Switzerland.text: A point against Canada secures a top-two finish.
Switzerland controls its own fate against Canada; only a loss brings risk.

breakdown.text: Second or third, decided by Bosnia–Qatar and goal difference.

Switzerland.text: If the match is drawn, Canada's superior goal difference (+6 to +3) takes first, leaving Switzerland second.

Bosnia and Herzegovina.text: Mathematically alive, but it needs to beat Qatar and see the top two stumble.
Only a win gives any chance, and even then Canada or Switzerland must drop points.

breakdown.text: A faint top-two or best-third hope, dependent on the other match.

Bosnia and Herzegovina.text: A win would lift them to four points; a best-third place on that tally is plausible, but it depends on the other groups.

Qatar.text: A long shot: Qatar needs a heavy win over Bosnia and Herzegovina and favours elsewhere.
Only a win keeps a sliver of hope alive, and a -6 goal difference makes even that steep.

breakdown.text: A faint chance, needing the top two to slip.

overview.line: A point against Scotland secures top two, with first in sight.
A draw with Haiti is enough to go through.
Beat Brazil to go through; less leaves it to the best-third race.
Eliminated; cannot reach the top three.

Brazil.text: A point against Scotland secures a top-two place.
Brazil's result against Scotland decides it; a draw is enough.

breakdown.text: Drops to second or third, settled on goal difference.

Brazil.text: First place is a fight with Morocco and Scotland. As winners Brazil would meet Group F's runner-up; as runner-up, Group F's winner.
Brazil and Morocco drew head-to-head, so if they finish level the order is set by goal difference, where Brazil's +3 leads Morocco's +1.

Morocco.text: A draw against Haiti guarantees a top-two finish.
Morocco's game with Haiti is decisive; a point clinches it.

breakdown.text: Through, in the hunt for first.
Second or third, riding on the Scotland–Brazil result.

Morocco.text: Level with Brazil on points would go to goal difference (Brazil +3, Morocco +1); the head-to-head was a 1-1 draw.

Scotland.text: Scotland must beat Brazil to be sure of going through.
A win over Brazil sends Scotland through, maybe top of the group; anything less drops it into the best-third race.
A draw or loss leaves Scotland third on three points — currently inside the qualifying eight, but the later groups can still overtake it.

Haiti.text: No points and a -4 goal difference after two defeats: Haiti cannot reach the top three. Even beating Morocco leaves them last, behind Scotland on the head-to-head.

overview.line: Through as group winners; first place is already secured.
Beat Paraguay and second place is theirs.
Beat Australia and second place is theirs.
Eliminated; cannot reach the top three.

United States.text: Through to the round of 32 as group winners.
First place is locked; the match with Türkiye cannot change it. As winners, the United States meet one of the best third-placed teams.

Australia.text: A win over Paraguay guarantees second place.
It comes down to the head-to-head with Paraguay; the United States are out of reach for first.

breakdown.text: Second or third, decided on goal difference.

Australia.text: A draw leaves Australia and Paraguay level on points and head-to-head, so goal difference separates them — Australia starts at +0 to Paraguay's -2.
Third place would come on four points after a draw, likely enough, or three after a loss, which would be borderline.

Paraguay.text: A win over Australia guarantees second place.
The head-to-head with Australia decides second; first is gone to the United States.

breakdown.text: Second or third, on goal difference.

Paraguay.text: A draw ties Paraguay with Australia on points and head-to-head; goal difference decides, and Paraguay's -2 trails Australia's +0.
A best-third place is within reach on three or four points, but Paraguay's weaker goal difference makes it less secure than its rivals'.

Türkiye.text: Two defeats, no points, a -3 goal difference: Türkiye cannot climb out of fourth. Having lost to both Australia and Paraguay, even beating the United States leaves them bottom on the head-to-head.

overview.line: Through as group winners; first place is already secured.
A point against Curaçao guarantees a top-two finish.
Must beat Germany and hope the other match falls right.
A long shot: beat Côte d'Ivoire and overturn a -6 goal difference.

Germany.text: Through to the round of 32 as group winners.
First place is secured whatever happens against Ecuador. As winners, Germany meet one of the best third-placed teams.

Côte d'Ivoire.text: A draw or win against Curaçao secures qualification.
Côte d'Ivoire's own result against Curaçao settles it; Germany are uncatchable for first.

breakdown.text: Out of the top two, into the best-third race.

Côte d'Ivoire.text: A loss would leave them third on three points — around the cutoff, so it would depend on the other groups.

Ecuador.text: Alive, but only by beating Germany.
A win over Germany is needed, paired with the right result between Curaçao and Côte d'Ivoire.

breakdown.text: Into the second-place contest, decided by the other match and goal difference.

Ecuador.text: A points tie for second would come down to goal difference, which the final scores can still swing.

Curaçao.text: A long shot: Curaçao must beat Côte d'Ivoire and erase a -6 goal difference.
Only a win keeps Curaçao alive, and even then the Ecuador–Germany result and a heavy goal swing are needed.

breakdown.text: A faint top-two chance, dependent on the other match.

Curaçao.text: Even level on points, Curaçao's -6 goal difference sits far behind, so only a big win changes the picture.

overview.line: A point against Tunisia guarantees a top-two place.
A point against Sweden guarantees a top-two place.
Beat Japan to go through; otherwise it's the best-third race.
Eliminated; cannot reach the top three.

Netherlands.text: A point against Tunisia secures a top-two finish.
The Netherlands control it against Tunisia; only a defeat introduces risk.

breakdown.text: Second or third, depending on Japan–Sweden and goal difference.

Netherlands.text: First place is level with Japan on points and goal difference, so the final results decide the order. The group winner meets Group C's runner-up; the runner-up meets Group C's winner.
Netherlands and Japan are level on points and goal difference, and drew head-to-head; if they stay tied, total goals scored is the next decider (Netherlands 7, Japan 6).

Japan.text: A point against Sweden secures a top-two finish.
Japan's game with Sweden is decisive; a draw is enough.

breakdown.text: Through, in the race for first.
Second or third, decided on goal difference.

Japan.text: Japan is level with the Netherlands on points, goal difference and head-to-head; if that holds, goals scored decides, where Japan's 6 trails the Netherlands' 7.

Sweden.text: Sweden must beat Japan to be sure of going through.
A win over Japan takes Sweden through, perhaps top of the group; anything less means the best-third race.
On three points Sweden currently sits top of the third-placed teams on goals scored, but later groups can still push it out.

Tunisia.text: Two heavy defeats, no points and a -8 goal difference: Tunisia cannot reach the top three even by beating the Netherlands.
